title: Show-CronAiAthonTool DataTable boolean cast failure on Enabled column from schedule JSON
description: Show-CronAiAthonTool.ps1 line 715 adds rows to an untyped DataTable: $dt.Rows.Add($t.id, $t.name, $t.type, $t.enabled, ...). The 'Enabled' column (line 710) is added without a type via $dt.Columns.Add('Enabled'). When $t.enabled is a boolean from ConvertFrom-Json (PSObject-wrapped), the DataTable infers the column type as Boolean from the first row. Subsequent rows with PSObject-wrapped booleans fail with 'System.InvalidCastException: Unable to cast PSObject to IConvertible' at BooleanStorage.Set. The Main-GUI.ps1 Add-FolderRow at line 391 passes $false directly to a CheckBoxColumn Rows.Add which is less risky but can fail in edge cases.
resolution: All 7 DataTable columns typed as [string], all Rows.Add values cast to [string] including $t.enabled.
detection_method: User-reported 'Unable to cast PSObject to IConvertible' at BooleanStorage.Set. Grep scan of .Columns.Add and .Rows.Add identified untyped columns receiving PSObject-wrapped values from JSON deserialization.
root_cause: DataTable columns added without explicit type parameter inherit type from first value. PowerShell PSObject wrapping of ConvertFrom-Json values causes .NET type coercion failures at the Storage layer.
exception_message: System.InvalidCastException: Unable to cast object of type 'System.Management.Automation.PSObject' to type 'System.IConvertible' at System.Data.Common.BooleanStorage.Set(Int32 record, Object value)
